description: This optical micrograph displays the fatigue crack behavior in specimen BL (longitudinal direction) of alloy B (Fe-29Mn-9.1Al-0.6C), which contains approximately 10% ferrite and 90% austenite after solution treatment at 1050°C. The image reveals fatigue cracks that initiated at slip bands within the austenite matrix but were subsequently impeded or arrested by the ferrite phase grains. The ferrite phase appears as bright stringers or bands oriented perpendicular to the crack propagation direction, effectively acting as crack arresters. A scale bar of 50 μm is present. This micrograph directly supports the finding that when the string arrangement of ferrite is perpendicular to the crack propagation direction (as in specimen BL), the soft ferrite grains impede crack growth through crack blunting and work hardening mechanisms, resulting in superior fatigue resistance compared to specimen BT where ferrite strings are parallel to crack propagation.
